skill.name: extract
skill.description: Extract and consolidate reusable components, design tokens, and patterns into your design system. Identifies opportunities for systematic reuse and enriches your component library.
skill.summary: Extract and consolidate reusable components, design tokens, and patterns into your design system. Id...
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: All 19 static findings are false positives. The skill is a benign design system extraction tool that analyzes code for reusable patterns. The flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns are simply markdown text in descriptions and instructions (e.g., YAML frontmatter, section headers). No actual cryptographic code or security vulnerabilities exist.

content.value_statement: Identify and extract reusable components, design tokens, and patterns from your codebase into a structured design system. This skill consolidates scattered UI code into systematic, documented assets.
actual_capabilities: Analyze codebase to locate design systems and identify extraction opportunities
Find repeated components, hard-coded values, and inconsistent implementations
Create reusable components with proper APIs, variants, and accessibility
Generate design tokens with semantic naming and documentation
Systematically migrate existing code to use new shared components
Update design system documentation with new components and examples
limitations: Cannot create a design system from scratch without existing code patterns
Requires existing design system structure to follow conventions
Does not handle non-web frameworks (mobile, desktop) without adaptation
Cannot validate visual output or run visual regression tests

use_cases.title: Component Consolidation
use_cases.description: Find and extract repeated button, card, input, and other UI patterns scattered across the codebase into a single component library.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers working on growing codebases

use_cases.title: Design Token Creation
use_cases.description: Extract hard-coded colors, spacing, typography, and shadow values into a structured design token system.
use_cases.target_user: Design system maintainers and UI engineers

use_cases.title: Migration Automation
use_cases.description: Systematically replace old implementations with new design system components while maintaining functionality.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Components from Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract skill to find reusable patterns in the authentication feature. Look for repeated buttons, form inputs, and layout patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a specific feature area to analyze

prompt_templates.title: Build Design Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Run extract to identify all hard-coded color values, spacing units, and typography styles in the codebase and organize them into design tokens.
prompt_templates.scenario: When building a design system from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Consolidate UI Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Use extract to analyze the ui/ directory, identify components that should be shared, and create a plan for consolidating them.

prompt_templates.title: Full Design System Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run extract without a target to scan the entire codebase, identify all extraction opportunities, and prioritize by usage frequency and maintenance benefit.
prompt_templates.scenario: When performing a comprehensive design system audit

output: Found 3 button implementations across the codebase:
Recommendation: Create unified Button component with variant prop
- 13 additional colors for specific use cases
Created tokens: color-primary, color-secondary, color-success, color-warning, color-error

best_practices: Extract patterns that are used 3+ times or clearly have reuse potential
Follow existing design system conventions for naming and structure
Always create proper TypeScript types and documentation for new components
anti_patterns: Creating overly generic components that are hard to use
Skipping accessibility (ARIA, keyboard navigation) in extracted components

faq.question: What is the extract skill?
faq.answer: Extract is a Claude skill that identifies reusable components, design tokens, and patterns in your codebase and consolidates them into a structured design system for systematic reuse.

faq.question: Does extract work with any framework?
faq.answer: Extract analyzes code patterns conceptually and can work with React, Vue, Angular, and other web frameworks. It identifies patterns regardless of the specific framework used.

faq.question: Can extract create a design system from nothing?
faq.answer: No, extract needs existing code patterns to analyze. It cannot create a design system from scratch but can help build one by extracting from existing implementations.

faq.question: How does extract handle accessibility?
faq.answer: Extract includes accessibility best practices in extracted components, including ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and focus management recommendations.

faq.question: Will extract break my existing code?
faq.answer: Extract creates new components alongside existing ones and provides a migration plan. It does not automatically modify existing code to prevent breaking changes.

faq.question: What tools does extract work with?
faq.answer: Extract works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It uses file reading and writing capabilities to analyze and create components.
